title: centaurea bethurica
intro: Centaurea bethurica, also known as White Cockle, is a flowering plant in the daisy family native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, where it has been traditionally used as a treatment for diarrhea and dysentery due to its anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Centaurea bethurica include its ability to soothe and calm digestive issues, and it is also used in herbal preparations to treat fever and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often dried and made into tea, capsules, or tinctures for its medicinal benefits.
